reforms.description: The Swiss government has implemented several federal programmes aimed at promoting R&D and training, as well as the diffusion of new technologies, crucial for industrial development. Additionally, a research assistance appropriation for the national research fund was approved, totaling SF739 million for the period 1984-87, alongside a commitment of SF150 million for various research initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding and support for R&D and training are expected to enhance industrial development and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been explicitly stated as implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant appropriation for the national research fund indicates a major reform in public R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: The so-called 'impulse' programmes which were discussed in the 1984 OECD Economic Survey of Switzerland include the promotion of R & D and training and the diffusion of new technologies which are important for industrial development.
